attacking_style: Tends to operate conservatively, avoiding ostentatious displays of power. Capable of stealth positioning to finish high on GC stages without drawing attention before key TT stages.
team_tactics: Movistar employs fluid hierarchy; willing to sacrifice Rodriguez's breakaway position when stronger teammates (e.g., Rubio) attack. Operates within multi-leader structure rather than receiving full team commitment.
terrain_preference: Performs well on mixed mountain stages with time trial components. Capable on multi-climb stages but does not dominate steep gradients.
vulnerability: Subordinated within team tactics when other riders show superior form. May be bypassed in favor of stage-hunting teammates even when already positioned in breakaways.
key_patterns: Conceals form on mountain stages, finishing efficiently without visible effort before TT stages.
Functions within flexible Movistar team hierarchy, accepting tactical subordination to stronger teammates.
Positioned for GC placement rather than stage wins in stage races.
form_trajectory: neutral: Limited recent data, but showed solid GC capability at Tour de Suisse while accepting support role at Romandie
